articles.title: ‘The Daily Show’ Tackles Trump’s Latest Move in His ‘War on Canada’
articles.description: “Fighting again with Canada?” Desi Lydic said about the president imposing new tariffs on the country. “I thought we were going to work this ‘oot.’”

articles.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
articles.description: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

articles.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
articles.description: The risk of continued fighting and a threatened blockade in the Red Sea added to uncertainty about the flow of energy from the region.

articles.title: Brent oil price hovers near $95 as Rubio says U.S. will 'continue to protect shipping' in Hormuz
articles.description: The U.S. has launched strikes against Iran for 11 consecutive days, citing a need to "degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial shipping."

articles.title: A Wartime Rift in Ukraine
articles.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky fired his top general after a feud between old-school officers and younger innovators spilled into the streets.

articles.title: What is Pickaxe Mountain, the Iranian nuclear facility Trump is threatening to strike?
articles.description: "We'll be hitting that area pretty soon, and very heavily," President Donald Trump said about Iran's underground nuclear facility in Pickaxe Mountain.

articles.title: Saudis Reach Deal With U.S. That Could Let Them Enrich Nuclear Fuel
articles.description: U.S. lawmakers and Israeli officials have expressed opposition to such a plan, to be announced Wednesday, fearing that the kingdom could use a civilian nuclear project to develop nuclear weapons.

articles.title: Iran War Live Updates: Rubio Signals No Letup in U.S. Strikes
articles.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that the United States would continue attacking Iran as long as it tried to control the Strait of Hormuz, and suggested that Tehran was “not serious” about diplomacy.

articles.title: Trump’s New Trade Fights (and Deals)
articles.description: President Trump has resumed his approach of big threats against some trading partners and generous pacts with others. Experts see new risks.

articles.title: Ukraine’s New Military Chief Faces a Monumental Test
articles.description: Maj. Gen. Mykhailo Drapatyi faces a balancing act between asserting the independence favored by military reformers and accommodating political input on battlefield strategy.

articles.title: Expected U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Fuels Israeli Concerns of Regional Arms Race
articles.description: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the deal, but critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

articles.title: A Lettuce-Growing Region in Mexico Stomachs the Fallout From the Cyclospora Outbreak
articles.description: In central Mexico, a lettuce-growing region has been potentially linked to an outbreak in the U.S. that has sickened thousands.

articles.title: Dirty Lettuce
articles.description: We look into the cyclospora outbreak.

articles.title: Growing trend of hating men backfires as anti-marriage culture leaves single women losing out
articles.description: With U.S. marriage rates falling and hookup culture alienating young women, a columnist warns that the anti-marriage movement has real consequences.

articles.title: Firing of Ukraine’s Top General Signals the End of an Era
articles.description: Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi, who commanded crucial battles, oversaw an expansion of drone warfare against Russia, but many Ukrainians want even more.

articles.title: Trump Reaches Contentious New Nuclear Deal, and A.I. Models Go Rogue
articles.description: Plus, what is a Jimothy?

articles.title: 6 Takeaways From Arizona’s Tense, Trump-Dominated Primaries
articles.description: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

articles.title: See How Houthis Put the Red Sea at Risk as an Alternative Oil Route
articles.description: Saudi Arabia has diverted huge amounts of oil to the Red Sea since the Iran war began, but now the Iranian-backed Houthi militants say that they intend to block that route.

articles.title: At a Utah Campus, an Emotional Odyssey After Charlie Kirk’s Death
articles.description: Since Mr. Kirk was gunned down at Utah Valley University, the school and its students have struggled to find a way forward.

articles.title: See Which 52 Places to Go We’ve Already Visited From Warsaw to Bangkok
articles.description: Now halfway through the year, we’ve made it to 11 of our picks, uncovering lesser-known spots where the American Revolution was fought, all-night parties in Poland and more.

articles.title: How Donald Trump Jr.’s 1789 Capital Is Cashing In Without Apology
articles.description: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

articles.title: Experts finally solve mystery of teenage Revolutionary War soldier 246 years after his death
articles.description: DNA analysis has identified Private John Pumphrey, a teenager from a prominent Maryland family who died at the Battle of Camden in South Carolina in 1780.

articles.title: When New York City comes second: The problem with an Israel-first mayor
articles.description: New York City elected a mayor to fix housing, crime and schools, but Zohran Mamdani's defining obsession appears to be Israel, not the five boroughs.

articles.title: Man Who Attacked Salman Rushdie Faces Terrorism Charges in Federal Trial
articles.description: Mr. Rushdie is expected to testify against Hadi Matar, the man who repeatedly stabbed him in 2022. Mr. Matar’s lawyers argue that he acted on his own, not on behalf of Iran.

articles.title: Hegseth turns tables on Dems as sparks fly during hearing clash: 'I put this on you'
articles.description: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth clashed with Democratic senators over Iran war strategy and military funding amid a record $1.5 trillion budget request.

articles.title: Wildfire Threatening Pleasanton Prompts Evacuation Order for Thousands
articles.description: One firefighter was injured as a fire spread quickly across dry hillsides in Northern California, forcing some residents to evacuate.

articles.title: Campus conflict exposed at the core of SJSU's messy fight vs Trump admin over volleyball scandal
articles.description: Exclusive videos and documents reveal SJSU torn apart internally over the Blaire Fleming volleyball scandal as trans activists confront administrators.

articles.title: Cyclospora outbreak: Is it still safe to eat at restaurants? Here's what to know
articles.description: A food policy professor reveals why diners shouldn't fear restaurants during the cyclospora outbreak but should take key precautions with leafy greens.

articles.title: State Department rips revolutionary socialist Nicaraguan regime as far-left leader ends country's elections
articles.description: Daniel Ortega declared Nicaragua will never hold elections again, prompting the State Department to condemn the Murillo-Ortega dictatorship.

articles.title: Kalshi launches election hub for prediction markets ahead of midterms
articles.description: The company said the page gives viewers of its event contracts a central place to see speculators' outlook for the midterm elections.

articles.title: Iran’s Neighbors Long for a Deal, Any Deal, to End the War
articles.description: Business owners in Pakistan are getting impatient for a chance to finally trade goods and oil along their long shared border with Iran.

articles.title: Rubio says US remains open to Iran talks but warns there 'will be consequences'
articles.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran faces consequences for violating commitments, saying Tehran demands illegal control over the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Zelenskyy ousts Ukraine’s army chief as protests expose wartime power struggle
articles.description: The reshuffle comes after almost a week of nationwide unrest and represents one of the biggest political crises since Russia's full-scale invasion in 2022.

articles.title: Trump plans generic drug tariffs from 2028 with two-year delay testing U.S. onshoring push
articles.description: The latest tariff salvo underscored Trump's goal of reshoring low-cost drug production, but displacing established suppliers like India won't be easy.

articles.title: State of play: Dems battle for shot at crucial Arizona House seat amid GOP's razor-thin majority
articles.description: Amish Shah and Marlene Galán-Woods are locked in a tight Arizona 1st congressional district primary rematch that could decide House control.

articles.title: Russia Will Not Yield
articles.description: A journey around the globe showed the country’s relentless adaptation.

articles.title: India steps up energy shift as costs and tariff pressures mount — oil blending push sparks backlash
articles.description: India is banking on alternative fuels as it prioritizes energy security amid high oil prices and supply constraints.

articles.title: Tehran's Hormuz threat risks global fallout, Rubio warns as U.S. strikes Iran for 11th straight night
articles.description: His comments come as U.S. Central Command hit Iranian targets for an 11th straight night.

articles.title: Joe McCarthy Lives!
articles.description: Trump’s attempt to drum up a third red scare is certain to fail.

articles.title: For Bats, New York’s a Wonderful Town
articles.description: It looks as though the bat population is rising in the city, where the creatures have bounced back after a disease lessened their ranks some years back.

articles.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard of
articles.description: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

articles.title: A Hiker Confronts His Fear of Heights at Ausable Chasm in Upstate N.Y.
articles.description: A jittery hiker signed up for a via ferrata tour on the cliffs of an Adirondacks gorge. Here’s what he learned on the ledges, rungs and exposed bridges.

articles.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
articles.description: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

articles.title: The Enduring Popularity of ‘the Surrendered Wife’
articles.description: The best-selling author Laura Doyle believes women can be too controlling. Many women — of all types — credit her with saving their marriages.

articles.title: Germany’s Military Buyers Look to Reduce Caution and Embrace Drones
articles.description: As threats from Moscow rise and support from Washington dwindles, the German military is reinventing how it researches, funds and wields 21st-century arms.
